content.text:
You are building a local-only Residential Life Operations Portal in this repo.

Tech stack:
- Frontend: React
- Backend: Spring Boot
- Database: PostgreSQL
- Network model: fully offline/on-prem

Rules:
- Keep responses short and implementation-focused.
- Make code changes directly in the repo.
- Prefer simple, maintainable structure over cleverness.
- Preserve existing work.
- After changes, print:
  1. files changed
  2. how to run
  3. any unfinished items
 Using the existing repo as a blank starting point, scaffold a full-stack monorepo for:
- `frontend/` React app
- `backend/` Spring Boot app
- local development with PostgreSQL

Requirements:
- Keep setup minimal but production-shaped.
- Add README with run steps.
- Add `.gitignore`.
- Add a docker compose file for PostgreSQL only.
- Backend should expose `/api/health`.
- Frontend should render an app shell with navigation placeholders.

Do not build features yet. Just create a clean foundation and folder structure.

content.name: Write
content.arguments:
{"file_path": "/home/leul/Documents/w2t63/repo/docker-compose.yml", "content": "version: '3.9'

services:
  postgres:
    image: postgres:16-alpine
    container_name: reslife_db
    environment:
      POSTGRES_DB: reslife
      POSTGRES_USER: reslife
      POSTGRES_PASSWORD: reslife
    ports:
      - "5432:5432"
    volumes:
      - pgdata:/var/lib/postgresql/data

volumes:
  pgdata:
"}

volumes:

content.name: Write
content.arguments:
{"file_path": "/home/leul/Documents/w2t63/repo/README.md", "content": "# Residential Life Operations Portal

Local-only, on-prem portal for managing residential life operations.

## Stack

| Layer    | Technology          |
|----------|---------------------|
| Frontend | React 18            |
| Backend  | Spring Boot 3 / Java 21 |
| Database | PostgreSQL 16       |

## Prerequisites

- Docker + Docker Compose
- Java 21
- Maven 3.9+
- Node 20+

## Running locally

### 1. Start PostgreSQL

```bash
docker compose up -d
```

### 2. Start the backend

```bash
cd backend
./mvnw spring-boot:run
```

Verify: `curl http://localhost:8080/api/health` → `{"status":"ok"}`

### 3. Start the frontend

```bash
cd frontend
npm install
npm start
```

Opens at http://localhost:3000. API calls are proxied to `localhost:8080`.

## Project structure

```
.
├── docker-compose.yml       # PostgreSQL only
├── frontend/                # React app
│   ├── public/
│   └── src/
│       ├── App.js           # App shell + navigation
│       ├── components/      # Shared components (empty)
│       └── pages/           # Page components (empty)
└── backend/                 # Spring Boot app
    └── src/main/java/com/reslife/api/
        ├── ReslifeApplication.java
        └── controller/
            └── HealthController.java
```

## Database connection

Default credentials (local dev only):

| Field    | Value       |
|----------|-------------|
| Host     | localhost   |
| Port     | 5432        |
| Database | reslife     |
| User     | reslife     |
| Password | reslife     |
"}

content.text: The file /home/leul/Documents/w2t63/repo/README.md has been updated successfully.

---

**Files changed:**
```
.gitignore
docker-compose.yml
README.md
backend/pom.xml
backend/mvnw  (+chmod +x)
backend/.mvn/wrapper/maven-wrapper.properties
backend/src/main/resources/application.properties
backend/src/main/java/com/reslife/api/ReslifeApplication.java
backend/src/main/java/com/reslife/api/controller/HealthController.java
frontend/package.json
frontend/public/index.html
frontend/src/index.js
frontend/src/App.js
```

**How to run:**
```bash
# 1. Database
docker compose up -d

# 2. Backend (needs Java 21 + Maven)
cd backend && mvn spring-boot:run

# 3. Frontend (needs Node 20+)
cd frontend && npm install && npm start
```

**Unfinished items:**
- Maven not installed on this machine — `./mvnw` will fall back to system `mvn`; install Maven 3.9+ or Java 21 to run the backend
- `frontend/src/components/` and `frontend/src/pages/` are empty — ready for features
- No auth, no CORS config, no DB migrations (Flyway/Liquibase) — add when needed

Extend the Spring Boot backend with an initial domain model and PostgreSQL schema for:
- users
- roles
- students/residents
- emergency contacts
- move-in records
- housing agreements
- message threads
- messages
- notifications
- configuration versions
- audit logs

Requirements:
- Use clear entity boundaries.
- Add migrations.
- Include created/updated timestamps.
- Add soft delete support where appropriate.
- Keep fields aligned with the portal requirements.

Do not implement every endpoint yet. Focus on schema, entities, repositories, and basic service structure.

Perfect! Here's the complete file tree and the contents of the requested files:

## Complete File Tree

```
/home/leul/Documents/w2t63/repo/backend/
├── mvnw
├── .mvn/
│   └── wrapper/
│       └── maven-wrapper.properties
├── pom.xml
└── src/
    └── main/
        ├── java/
        │   └── com/
        │       └── reslife/
        │           └── api/
        │               ├── ReslifeApplication.java
        │               └── controller/
        │                   └── HealthController.java
        └── resources/
            └── application.properties
```

## pom.xml Contents

```xml
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<project xmlns="http://maven.apache.org/POM/4.0.0"
         xmlns:xsi="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance"
         xsi:schemaLocation="http://maven.apache.org/POM/4.0.0 https://maven.apache.org/xsd/maven-4.0.0.xsd">
  <modelVersion>4.0.0</modelVersion>

  <parent>
    <groupId>org.springframework.boot</groupId>
    <artifactId>spring-boot-starter-parent</artifactId>
    <version>3.2.5</version>
    <relativePath/>
  </parent>

  <groupId>com.reslife</groupId>
  <artifactId>reslife-api</artifactId>
  <version>0.0.1-SNAPSHOT</version>
  <name>reslife-api</name>

  <properties>
    <java.version>21</java.version>
  </properties>

  <dependencies>
    <dependency>
      <groupId>org.springframework.boot</groupId>
      <artifactId>spring-boot-starter-web</artifactId>
    </dependency>
    <dependency>
      <groupId>org.springframework.boot</groupId>
      <artifactId>spring-boot-starter-data-jpa</artifactId>
    </dependency>
    <dependency>
      <groupId>org.postgresql</groupId>
      <artifactId>postgresql</artifactId>
      <scope>runtime</scope>
    </dependency>
    <dependency>
      <groupId>org.springframework.boot</groupId>
      <artifactId>spring-boot-starter-test</artifactId>
      <scope>test</scope>
    </dependency>
  </dependencies>

  <build>
    <plugins>
      <plugin>
        <groupId>org.springframework.boot</groupId>
        <artifactId>spring-boot-maven-plugin</artifactId>
      </plugin>
    </plugins>
  </build>
</project>
```

## application.properties Contents

```properties
spring.application.name=reslife-api
server.port=8080

spring.datasource.url=jdbc:postgresql://localhost:5432/reslife
spring.datasource.username=reslife
spring.datasource.password=reslife

spring.jpa.hibernate.ddl-auto=update
spring.jpa.show-sql=false
spring.jpa.properties.hibernate.dialect=org.hibernate.dialect.PostgreSQLDialect
```

This is a Spring Boot 3.2.5 application written in Java 21, configured to connect to a PostgreSQL database locally. The project uses JPA/Hibernate for ORM and includes standard Spring Boot web and test dependencies.

## pom.xml Contents

```xml
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<project xmlns="http://maven.apache.org/POM/4.0.0"
         xmlns:xsi="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance"
         xsi:schemaLocation="http://maven.apache.org/POM/4.0.0 https://maven.apache.org/xsd/maven-4.0.0.xsd">
  <modelVersion>4.0.0</modelVersion>

  <parent>
    <groupId>org.springframework.boot</groupId>
    <artifactId>spring-boot-starter-parent</artifactId>
    <version>3.2.5</version>
    <relativePath/>
  </parent>

  <groupId>com.reslife</groupId>
  <artifactId>reslife-api</artifactId>
  <version>0.0.1-SNAPSHOT</version>
  <name>reslife-api</name>

  <properties>
    <java.version>21</java.version>
  </properties>

  <dependencies>
    <dependency>
      <groupId>org.springframework.boot</groupId>
      <artifactId>spring-boot-starter-web</artifactId>
    </dependency>
    <dependency>
      <groupId>org.springframework.boot</groupId>
      <artifactId>spring-boot-starter-data-jpa</artifactId>
    </dependency>
    <dependency>
      <groupId>org.postgresql</groupId>
      <artifactId>postgresql</artifactId>
      <scope>runtime</scope>
    </dependency>
    <dependency>
      <groupId>org.springframework.boot</groupId>
      <artifactId>spring-boot-starter-test</artifactId>
      <scope>test</scope>
    </dependency>
  </dependencies>

  <build>
    <plugins>
      <plugin>
        <groupId>org.springframework.boot</groupId>
        <artifactId>spring-boot-maven-plugin</artifactId>
      </plugin>
    </plugins>
  </build>
</project>
```

## application.properties Contents

```properties
spring.application.name=reslife-api
server.port=8080

spring.datasource.url=jdbc:postgresql://localhost:5432/reslife
spring.datasource.username=reslife
spring.datasource.password=reslife

spring.jpa.hibernate.ddl-auto=update
spring.jpa.show-sql=false
spring.jpa.properties.hibernate.dialect=org.hibernate.dialect.PostgreSQLDialect

This is a Spring Boot 3.2.5 application written in Java 21, configured to connect to a PostgreSQL database locally. The project uses JPA/Hibernate for ORM and includes standard Spring Boot web and test dependencies.
